title: Suicide Precautions: Maintaining a Safe Environment
subtitle: A 32-year-old is admitted to inpatient psychiatry with active suicidal ideation and a plan. The nurse establishes suicide precautions — the right observation level, means restriction, a ligature-safe room, and a searched environment.

patient.name: Jordan Wells
patient.admitDate: Inpatient psychiatry for active suicidal ideation with a plan and a hopeless affect. Placed on suicide precautions: continuous 1:1 observation, means restriction, ligature-safe room, and a searched environment.

vitals.temp: Active SI with a plan; hopeless affect

nursesNote: Jordan Wells, 32, admitted with active suicidal ideation and a plan; placed on suicide precautions. Environmental safety is central: maintain the ordered observation level (continuous 1:1 = arm's-length, including the bathroom), restrict means, and make the room ligature-safe (remove cords/belts/shoelaces/drawstrings). Search belongings and secure contraband (sharps, medications, glass, lighters); supervise meals with plastic utensils. Perform safety checks at random, unpredictable intervals; never leave a 1:1 patient alone. Ask directly about ideation/plan/means (it does not plant the idea), engage the patient in a safety plan, and reassess risk regularly and at every hand-off. A 'no-suicide contract' does not replace monitoring.
providerOrders: Initiate suicide precautions; place on the ordered observation level (1:1)
Search belongings; remove/secure ligatures, sharps, medications, and contraband
Provide a ligature-safe environment; supervise meals (plastic utensils)
Perform safety checks at random intervals; never leave a 1:1 patient alone
Reassess risk regularly; engage the patient in a safety plan; document/communicate

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What does this patient's safety require?

opts.text: A patient with active suicidal ideation and a plan is at high risk
Environmental safety (means restriction) is central to suicide prevention
Common ligature risks include cords, belts, shoelaces, and drawstrings
Observation levels range from q15-minute checks to continuous 1:1
Asking directly about suicide plants the idea and should be avoided
Belongings should be searched and dangerous items secured
1:1 observation is continuous, arm's-length supervision, including the bathroom
Safety checks should occur at random, unpredictable intervals
Asking directly about ideation, plan, and means does NOT increase risk
Medications, sharps, glass, and cords must be kept away from the patient
A signed "no-suicide contract" reliably prevents suicide
Risk should be reassessed regularly and at any status change
Assess suicide risk (ideation, plan, means, intent)
Determine and initiate the appropriate observation level
Make the environment safe (remove ligatures, search, secure contraband)
Engage the patient in a safety plan and restrict means
Reassess risk, document, and communicate at hand-off

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.sub: Does the action reduce or increase suicide risk?

opts.text: Remove cords, belts, shoelaces, and drawstrings
Conduct an admission search and secure contraband/sharps
Maintain continuous 1:1 observation, including in the bathroom
Perform safety checks at random, unpredictable intervals
Ask directly about suicidal thoughts, plan, and access to means
Provide plastic utensils and supervise meals
Leave the patient's medications at the bedside
Conduct checks at the same predictable time each round
Step away from a 1:1 patient while they use the bathroom

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Place the patient on the ordered observation level without gaps
Search belongings and remove cords, sharps, meds, and other hazards
Use a safe room and supervise meals with plastic utensils
Perform checks at random intervals and stay with a 1:1 patient
Reassess risk, update the plan, and document/communicate
Ligature risks are removed and the room is made safe
Continuous 1:1 observation is maintained without gaps
The patient engages in the safety plan and means are restricted
Risk is reassessed regularly and changes are communicated
A 1:1 patient is left alone and attempts self-harm
Contraband (a razor) is missed during the admission search
Predictable q15-minute checks let the patient anticipate gaps
A "no-suicide contract" is used in place of actual monitoring
